items.label: Balance & Clean
items.description: Tight, reliable filter profile with even sweetness and a clean finish.
notes: Use as the baseline when dialing a new coffee.
Prioritize even agitation and repeatable pour height.

items.label: More Sweetness
items.description: Push extraction slightly deeper for rounder sweetness and softer acidity.
notes: Finer grind and slightly longer contact time raise sweetness.
Avoid over-agitation late in the brew.

items.label: More Acidity
items.description: Open aromatics and brighter acids while keeping the cup transparent.
notes: Coarser grind and a shorter brew highlight top notes and florals.
Keep the bloom disciplined to prevent channeling.

items.label: More Body
items.description: Lean toward texture and structure with slightly denser extraction.
notes: Finer grind with slightly tighter ratio builds texture.
Control drawdown so the body feels syrupy, not muddy.

items.label: Floral & Transparent
items.description: Delicate high-altitude or washed coffees with a cleaner aromatic finish and lower agitation.
notes: Use for floral, washed, high-altitude, Geisha/Gesha, SL, and Ethiopian landrace cues.
Keep agitation calm and finish clean rather than pushing heavy extraction.

items.label: Fruit-Forward
items.description: Aroma-preserving profile for natural, anaerobic, carbonic, and high-aroma lots with conservative extraction pressure.
notes: Use when process cues suggest high aroma or fermentation variability.
Protect fruit-like aromatics without claiming added fruit ingredients unless the process text says so.

items.label: Soft & Round
items.description: Rounded sweetness profile for honey, pulped natural, medium roast, Brazil, nutty, and chocolate-leaning coffees.
notes: Use for soft sweetness and approachable texture without making the bed muddy.
Keep pour control even and avoid late heavy agitation.

items.label: Dense & Comforting
items.description: Body-forward profile for wet-hulled, canephora, liberica, excelsa, chocolate, spice, and low-clarity body cues.
notes: Build tactile body while keeping bitterness protected by controlled temperature and agitation.
Use clarity warnings when process or species cues are broad, earthy, or high-buffer water is selected.
